methodology: 10 apps were sampled from the 94 'first-pass' rows that had NOT yet been individually live-searched (these were populated from general documented technical knowledge, e.g. 'Stripe uses API keys'). Each sampled row was then checked against a live web search + the app's own docs page, exactly like the pipeline's fallback path would. A row is scored a MISS if any of {auth method, self-serve/gated status, or MCP/API-surface existence} was wrong or meaningfully incomplete.

results.first_pass_claim: Gated, no public API found, blocked
results.verified_finding: Has a live official MCP server (OAuth, self-serve free tier) plus a documented REST reference; only the high-volume/enterprise tier needs an application
Has a documented self-serve REST API (api.waterfall.io), API key issued immediately on signup

results.first_pass_claim: API is a paid add-on, no free key, gated
results.verified_finding: Confirmed on re-check: Ahrefs API requires a separate paid API subscription on top of a regular plan; no free/trial API key exists

results.first_pass_claim: Basic/Bearer auth, self-serve via free AuraDB tier, but the real interface is Cypher-over-Bolt not REST
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: AuraDB free tier is instant self-serve, and the primary query interface is the Bolt protocol/drivers rather than a general REST CRUD surface (Aura has a separate small management REST API)

results.first_pass_claim: Commerce API needs a paid Commerce-tier site
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: Squarespace's Commerce APIs require an active Commerce plan on the site; the lower-tier plans don't expose API keys for commerce endpoints

results.first_pass_claim: Gated behind an approved/funded Brex business account
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: Brex API credentials are only issued to live, underwritten business accounts -- there's no sandbox available without one
Largely confirmed, though Grain has since added a narrower Zapier-based path for lower tiers that softens the 'full block' framing slightly

results.first_pass_claim: Native API limited to Team/Enterprise plans, Zapier for lower tiers

results.verified_finding: Confirmed: API access is bundled starting at specific paid plan tiers, not available on the base/free trial

results.first_pass_claim: API/MCP gated behind a paid Devin plan, no free self-serve tier

scoring.after_correction_accuracy: 10/10 = 100% (2 rows fixed in the master dataset, 1 row given added nuance)
scoring.note: This 80% first-pass rate on a knowledge-only sample is the reason the assignment's verification loop matters: general technical knowledge about 'what auth does X use' is usually right for mainstream, stable APIs, but gets shakier for newer/smaller products (MCP servers ship monthly; smaller vendors' access models change without much announcement). The two misses were both apps that had shipped a new MCP server or API surface recently -- exactly the kind of thing a one-time training snapshot won't know and a live search catches immediately.
